label: hate
rationale: 'the Muslims will not stop until they kill them all' attributes genocidal intent collectively to Muslims as a group - NCIC-standard collective ethnic/religious contempt plus eliminationist framing.

label: hate
rationale: 'Nilotic Foreskin wielding retards' dehumanises and degrades a community collectively by ethnic/physical marker, going beyond any individual target.

label: offensive
rationale: 'THUG in chief', 'thugs and tribal bigots' - personal abuse of an individual leader and his circle, no protected characteristic targeted as a group.

label: neither
rationale: Advice to a politician to focus on Luo Nyanza while acknowledging Luhya politics - straightforward ethnic-bloc electoral analysis, no contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Criticism of Gachagua as a political liability and challenge to Kikuyu voters to reflect - targets a named politician and individual voters' choices, not the Kikuyu community as a whole.

label: neither
rationale: 'Embu's and Meru's are more lethal than those voters in Ol Kalau' - electoral-strength comparison between regional blocs, no contempt or attack.

label: neither
rationale: A challenge to cite specific statements against named ethnic groups - meta-discussion about evidence, no attack on any group.

label: neither
rationale: Alleges Kikuyu political manipulation of Luhyas and frames Sifuna as a stooge - harsh coalition criticism directed at political actors and strategy, closes with 'all communities are equal'; the ethnic framing is electoral, not contemptuous of any community as such.

label: neither
rationale: Strategic political advice to Ruto about Mt Kenya bloc loyalty and governing - extensive electoral analysis with no contempt attributed to any community as a group.

label: neither
rationale: 'Mulembe nation si wajinga nani' defends the Luhya community against perceived political manipulation - assertive community self-positioning in coalition talk, no attack on another group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Hata akili hawana, ni mafala wa mwisho' quoted as Ruto's words against Kalonzo - personal abuse of a named individual politician, no ethnic group targeted.

label: neither
rationale: Commentary on Cheruiyot and Murkomen's political positioning within Kalenjin sub-group dynamics - intra-community electoral analysis, no attack.

label: hate
rationale: 'deport all Zionists from our nation' - collective expulsion call targeting an ethno-religious group, a plain NCIC-standard exclusion demand.

label: neither
rationale: 'Kikuyus aren't stupid' is a defence of the community's political judgment, 'Uda chietha' is a slogan - overall the post is electoral analysis and community self-assertion, not contempt against another group.

label: hate
rationale: 'huwezi toboa na akili ya wamunyoro' collectively attributes inferior intelligence to a named ethnic community - NCIC-standard contemptuous negative characteristic attributed to a group.

label: neither
rationale: Criticises Ruto loyalists and tribalism as a political problem - 'ukabila ndio inawasumbua' condemns tribalism rather than enacting it; targets named politicians and political behaviour, not a community.

label: hate
rationale: 'occupy Laikipia county and do operation rudisha mzungu ulaya' - expulsion call targeting a racially identified group with collective removal framing.

label: neither
rationale: 'Ruto is as tribalist as Gachagua' - criticism of two named politicians' behaviour, not transferred onto any ethnic community.

label: neither
rationale: Argues Hamas and Muslim Brotherhood are responsible for Palestinian suffering - a political/geopolitical argument about organisations and groups; while contentious, the framing is critical of militant organisations rather than attributing collective negative characteristics to Muslims as an ethnic/religious group in NCIC terms.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Uwi jezebel zero brain kama bashite' - crude personal abuse of an individual (implied politician or public figure), no ethnic group targeted.

label: neither
rationale: Declares Luo support for named politicians and accuses an individual interlocutor of greed - intra-community political argument targeting a specific person, no contempt for any ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'RUTO MUST Rule kenya till 2032 whether KIKUYUS like it or NOT - By FIRE/BULLET' combines collective ethnic targeting with an explicit violence threat directed at Kikuyus as a community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Matiangi is a thief' - blunt personal insult at a named politician, no ethnic group implicated.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Deport them or shoot them in the face' is a sarcastic MAGA critique rather than a sincere expulsion/violence call against an ethnic group; the post mocks a political position rather than targeting a protected community - offensive in tone but no clear ethnic targeting.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kalenjin presidency is always a curse to the nation NEVER AGAIN' - verbatim match to example ex11; declares an entire ethnic community categorically unfit to lead, NCIC ethnic contempt.
